title: Undo, Restore, Revert, Reset, and Reflog Recovery · Repository Trace: Revert Public History
summary: Recover from an incorrect edit or commit using the least destructive operation and a verified recovery reference. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git revert public history increment in an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create and verify inverse commits for published changes, merges, and later dependencies without claiming history was erased.
Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Revert Public History
steps.instruction:
theory case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Revert Public History contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create and verify inverse commits for published changes, merges, and later dependencies without claiming history was erased.

content.text: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Revert Public History condition decided your prediction.

steps.title: Bound Revert Public History
steps.instruction:
theory case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Revert Public History application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create and verify inverse commits for published changes, merges, and later dependencies without claiming history was erased.

content.text: Create and verify inverse commits for published changes, merges, and later dependencies without claiming history was erased. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer. A defensible solution applies Revert Public History, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.

content.code:
git status --short
git branch recovery/before-repair
git diff
git reflog -n 4
# Choose restore, revert, or reset only after naming the affected layer.
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Reverting a merge permanently prevents all its changes from ever returning. This fails the Revert Public History contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Revert Public History evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-revert-public-history-1b3dbm" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Revert Public History into observable performance evidence.
